USD.catalyst: The U.S. dollar climbed to a one-week high on July 8, 2026, as renewed American military strikes on Iran reignited geopolitical tensions and fueled safe-haven demand. Fed 3.75%. Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh, speaking at the ECB Forum in Sintra on July 1, declined to provide specific guidance for the upcoming July rate decision but noted that inflation risks have moderated in recent weeks. Despite this, the dollar remains supported by a volatility risk premium as markets await the release of the FOMC Minutes against a backdrop of rising oil prices and geopolitical instability.

sources.title: Dollar at week-high after US resumes attacks on Iran - Reuters
Dollar heads for weekly drop as jobs data dims Fed hike bets - CNBC
Global shares mixed as US futures rise; oil slips after OPEC+ plans August output hike - The Sun Chronicle

GBP.catalyst: Sterling fell on July 6, snapping a seven-day rally as the U.S. dollar recovered from its post-payrolls selloff to trade near 1.3361. Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey joined a high-level policy panel in Sintra on July 1, though the pair has since been driven primarily by external dollar dynamics and a fading of domestic political risk premia. Technical support remains firm around the 20-day EMA, with bulls targeting a sustained move above 1.3400 as short positions continue to unwind. The currency remains sensitive to the broader safe-haven rotation triggered by the July 8 escalation in U.S.-Iran hostilities.

sources.title: Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh declines to hint at July rate decision - CNBC
Pound breaks seven-day rally as dollar steadies after jobs data - Reuters

EUR.catalyst: The Euro is struggling to maintain its position above the 1.1400 handle as fresh U.S. military action against Iran drives capital toward the dollar, offsetting earlier gains made during the Sintra policy panel. ECB President Christine Lagarde participated in the ECB Forum on Central Banking on July 1 alongside global peers, while ECB’s Fabio Panetta recently emphasized a fragile economic outlook that precludes a predetermined path for interest rates. European policymakers continue to diverge from the U.S. on digital currency strategy, with the ECB actively developing a retail digital euro while the U.S. Congress blocks a digital dollar until at least 2030. Current market sentiment is weighed down by an elevated volatility risk premium, with 1-month implied volatility reaching 7.1% as geopolitical headwinds persist.

sources.title: Europe And China Diverge From The U.S. On The Future Of Money - Forbes
Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh declines to hint at July rate decision - CNBC
Warsh drops inflation bombshell that could reset interest-rate bets - thestreet.com

JPY.catalyst: The Japanese yen has crashed to a 40-year low, breaching the 162.379 level and fueling intense speculation regarding unilateral intervention by the Ministry of Finance. Goldman Sachs analysts have revised their 12-month USD/JPY forecast to 165, citing Japan’s massive debt-to-GDP ratio of 240% and a fundamental shift in U.S. rate expectations as primary drivers of the slump. Internal Bank of Japan dynamics show a cautious stance, with dissenter Asada stating that demand-driven inflation must be firmly established before the central bank considers another rate hike. Despite its traditional role, the yen is failing to act as a safe haven during the current Iran conflict, with COT data showing heavy speculative net short positioning of -137,828 contracts.

sources.title: Yen to extend historic slump, while AI and energy 'supply bust' to support the U.S. dollar: Goldman - CNBC
Yen crashes as Japan’s debt crisis hits currency markets, and interventions are 'doomed to fail' - Fortune
The Japanese yen is at a 40-year low. Here’s why that matters - CNN
Safe havens aren't behaving like they used to. Here's what's changed - CNBC

AUD.catalyst: The Australian dollar is outperforming its G10 peers this week, supported by hawkish remarks from RBA’s Hunter regarding persistent inflationary pressures. The currency is also benefiting from significant M&A activity, notably a AUD 5.6 billion bid by Genesis Minerals for gold producer Vault Minerals announced on July 6. Local economic sentiment was further bolstered by reports that UFC 325 generated AUD 65.7 million in economic impact for the Sydney metro area. RBA 4.35%.

sources.title: Mining company Genesis bids 5.6 billion Australian dollars to acquire gold miner Vault - Bitget
UFC Delivers Knockout Results For Sydney - UFC.com

CAD.catalyst: The Canadian dollar reached a 10-day high of 1.4147 on July 2 following soft U.S. employment data, but it has since decoupled from its traditional correlation with energy markets. Despite WTI oil rising to $72.0, USD/CAD remains elevated near 1.4200, reflecting a severe correlation break of -2.08σ as USMCA renegotiation uncertainty weighs on the Bank of Canada’s rate hike prospects. Recent trade data showed a four-year high surplus, providing some fundamental support, but a July 3 Reuters poll indicates analysts are cutting CAD forecasts due to domestic growth concerns. The BOC’s latest survey also showed a decline in the balance of opinion on future sales indicators, falling to +15 from +24 in the first quarter.

sources.title: Analysts cut Canadian dollar forecasts as USMCA uncertainty clips rate hike chances - KITCO
Canadian dollar edges higher as trade data boosts growth outlook - KITCO
Canadian dollar hits 10-day high as US job growth slows - KITCO

CHF.catalyst: The Swiss Franc is currently trading in a tight range between its 100-hour and 200-hour moving averages as it loses its safe-haven edge to the U.S. dollar. While the currency saw some support at the end of last week, the July 8 escalation in the Middle East has seen the greenback outperform the franc in the traditional "flight to quality" rotation. Market participants are currently waiting for a technical breakout as political risks in neighboring Europe fade, reducing the immediate demand for Swiss neutrality. The USD/CHF pair remains the primary focus for traders looking to play the divergence in safe-haven efficacy during the current geopolitical crisis.

NZD.catalyst: The New Zealand dollar jumped on July 8 after the Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) unexpectedly raised the Official Cash Rate by 25 basis points to 2.50%. This policy shock, the first hike since 2023, was accompanied by hawkish guidance flagging further tightening to combat persistent inflation, catching markets off guard after Westpac had predicted a hold at 2.25%. The move triggered an acute short-squeeze, as speculative positioning was heavily net short (-25,757 contracts) leading into the decision. While the currency is vulnerable to broader risk-off sentiment, the RBNZ’s aggressive stance has established a significant yield advantage over several G10 peers.

sources.title: Dollar at week-high after U.S. resumes attacks on Iran - CNBC
Dollar at week-high after US resumes attacks on Iran - Reuters
Westpac: Reserve Bank of New Zealand has time to wait for more data - Bitget

NOK.catalyst: The Norwegian Krone remains range-bound and lacks specific domestic catalysts in recent sessions, as no major central bank officials have made public comments and high-tier data releases are absent. The currency continues to track broader moves in Brent crude oil and global risk sentiment, which has been volatile following the resumption of U.S. strikes on Iran. Without fresh policy signals from the Norges Bank, the krone is expected to fluctuate in line with the G10 beta to energy prices and geopolitical developments in the Middle East.

SEK.catalyst: The Swedish Krona is currently trading without significant domestic momentum, as recent headlines have focused on broader systemic warnings from the ESRB regarding AI-related financial vulnerabilities rather than Riksbank policy. The currency remains sensitive to the Eurozone’s economic outlook and global risk appetite, both of which are currently clouded by the escalation of hostilities between the U.S. and Iran. In the absence of specific Swedish inflation data or central bank speakers, the krona is largely following the lead of the EUR/USD cross and general safe-haven flows into the dollar.
